slide_006.appears_in: Work could be more meaningfully defined as an activity that produces something of value for other people.

slide_008.appears_in: Work and Self- Esteem Work contributes to self-esteem in at least two ways.

slide_009.appears_in: If your work doesn't provide you with any of these functions, you will often experience sensations of powerlessness, meaninglessness, and normlessness—a condition called alienation.

slide_010.appears_in: What is OB? Organizational behaviour (OB) is a field of study that investigates the impact individuals, groups, and structure have on behaviour within organizations, to apply such knowledge toward improving an organization's effectiveness.

slide_011.appears_in: OB's goal is to understand and predict human behaviour in organizations.

slide_012.appears_in: OB in Simple Terms The complexities of human behaviour are not easy to predict, but neither are they random—certain fundamental consistencies underlie the behaviour of all individuals.
OB studies three determinants of behaviour in organizations: individuals, groups, and structure.
OB in Simple Terms The complexities of human behaviour are not easy to predict, but neither are they random—certain fundamental consistencies underlie the behaviour of all individuals.
OB studies three determinants of behaviour in organizations: individuals, groups, and structure.

slide_014.appears_in: Multidisciplinary Nature of OB OB is an <u>applied behavioural science</u> built on contributions from several behavioural disciplines, mainly psychology and social psychology, sociology, anthropology, and, increasingly, political science and neuroscience.

slide_015.appears_in: -  Multidisciplinary Nature of OB lan af ¢ Sociology explores how society shapes people.
how individual experiences are part of broader : A ¢ Psychology seeks to measure, explain, and PSa sometimes change human behaviour.

slide_017.appears_in: Anthropology examines rites and rituals that shape how groups and cultures work.
Political science examines the role of power within organizations.
Multidisciplinary Nature of OB Social psychology bridges sociology and psychology, and tries to understand the impact the group has on the outlook of the individual.

slide_018.appears_in: Beginnings of OB Organizational behaviour as a distinct discipline started when scientific approaches to management began to indicate that behavioural considerations were important predictors of productivity.

slide_019.appears_in: Elton Mayo's and Fritz Roethlisberger's Hawthorne Studies is considered to be a milestone in Management Sciences.

slide_021.appears_in: Gradually, the researchers began to realize they had created a change in supervisory style and human relations, which they believed was the true cause of the increased productivity.

slide_022.appears_in: Employees performed better when i managers treated them in a_ positive eS manner ( Human relations).

slide_024.appears_in: In addition, Worker productivity increased partly as a result of the increased feeling of importance and 'group pride' employees felt by being selected for this important project.

slide_025.appears_in: Hawthorne effect: Subjects behaved differently because of the active participation of the researchers in the Hawthorne experiments.

slide_026.appears_in: Absolutes in OB Laws in the physical sciences—chemistry, astronomy, physics—are consistent and apply in various situations.
Absolutes in OB Laws in the physical sciences—chemistry, astronomy, physics—are consistent and apply in various situations.

slide_027.appears_in: The science of OB was developed by applying general concepts to a particular situation, person, or group.

slide_032.appears_in: This, in fact, is one of the most fascinating aspects of organisational and managerial work – and its essential ambiguity.

slide_033.appears_in: Management as an Integrating Activity Whatever the individual's orientation to work, the nature of the work organisation or cultural influences, it is through the process of management that the efforts of members of the organisation are coordinated, directed and guided towards the achievement of its goals.

Management as an Integrating Activity Whatever the individual's orientation to work, the nature of the work organisation or cultural influences, it is through the process of management that the efforts of members of the organisation are coordinated, directed and guided towards the achievement of its goals.

slide_034.appears_in: People bring their own perceptions, feelings and attitudes towards the organisation, systems and styles of management, their duties and responsibilities, and the conditions under which they are working.

slide_035.appears_in: Management as an Integrating Activity At the heart of successful management is the problem of integrating the individual and the organisation, and this requires an understanding of both human personality and work organisations.

slide_036.appears_in: The role of management as an integrating activity For example: Personality Skills and attributes Values and attributes Needs and expectations For example: Structure and functions Informal organisation Role relationships Group influences and pressure For example: Objectives and policy Technology and methods of work Formal structure Styles of leadership For example: Technical and scientific Economic Social and cultural Government Figure 1.6 Management as the cornerstone of organisational effectiveness

slide_037.appears_in: Use metrics and situational variables rather than "hunches" to explain cause-and-effect relationships.

slide_038.appears_in: OB can improve your employees' work quality and productivity by showing you how to empower your employees, design and implement change programs, improve customer service, and help your employees balance work-life conflicts.

slide_039.appears_in: Paying attention to relevant factors at all three levels rather than focusing on just one can improve management effectiveness.
Recognize that human behaviour in the workplace is influenced by factors at three different levels: individual, group, and organizational.
